commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds phone-only RootTabs state and Control hub callbacks so Chat opened from Overview, Activity, Workboard, or Sessions can show a contextual back action returning to that Control detail.

PR surface: Other +93. Total +93 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main routes phone Control detail Chat actions directly to the Chat tab and constructs phone Chat without a contextual leading action. I did not run an iOS simulator in this read-only review, so this is source-reproducible rather than locally reproduced.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A repair worker can make the focused source-guard/test-anchor fix without choosing product direction; maintainer review remains required afterward because the PR has the protected `maintainer` label.

target.title: fix(ios): chat can return to originating control screen
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where iPhone users who open Chat from a Control detail, including Sessions, can lose the visible path back to the Control screen they came from. This addresses the feedback that opening a "session" felt like a dead end while keeping the existing product model that a session is a chat.

## Why This Change Was Made

The phone Control hub now records the Control detail that launched Chat and passes Chat a contextual leading header action like "Back to Sessions" or "Back to Overview". Returning applies the pending Control destination even if the Control hub appears after the request, and the return state is cleared when the user leaves Chat through normal tab navigation.

This PR was AI-assisted. I reviewed the change, generated focused test coverage, ran the simulator proof below, and understand the behavior being changed.

## User Impact

On iPhone, users can move from Sessions, Overview, Activity, or Workboard into Chat and then use the visible back affordance to return to the originating Control detail. iPad/sidebar routing is unchanged.

## Evidence

- `pnpm ios:gen`
- `git diff --check`
- `xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClaw -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17' -only-testing:OpenClawTests/RootTabsPresentationTests test` passed: 44 tests in `RootTabsPresentationTests`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
- XcodeBuildMCP simulator run on `iPhone 17` with `--openclaw-screenshot-mode --openclaw-initial-tab control --openclaw-initial-destination activity` succeeded.
- Hosted proof bundle: https://github.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/tree/main/openclaw/pr-98023
- GIF proof:

![Activity Control detail opens Chat, then the contextual return affordance returns to Activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw/pr-98023/activity-chat-return.gif)

- MP4 proof: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw/pr-98023/activity-chat-return.mp4
- Contact sheet: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw/pr-98023/activity-chat-return-contact.png
- Runtime snapshot proof from the same recording flow:
  - Before: Activity detail exposed `e53|tap|button|Chat`.
  - Chat: destination exposed `e18|tap|button|Back to Activity||OpenClawChatBackToControlDetailButton`.
  - After tapping that target: the screen hash returned to Activity with `Activity`, `Gateway Online`, `Recent activity`, and the Control tab selected.
- Sessions-specific proof: the screenshot fixture only has the default `main` chat, and `CommandCenterTab.isRecentChatSession` filters that out, so there is no non-default session row to tap in the local fixture recording. The same implementation path is covered by `RootTabsPresentationTests`: `.sessions` maps to `Back to Sessions`, and `RootTabsPhoneControlHub` passes `.sessions` when opening Chat from `CommandSessionsScreen`.
target.author: solvely-colin

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
